content: The 2024-2025 SOTA push for scalable active memory in autonomous AI agents reveals that every AI agent’s long-term task performance is fundamentally limited by how it prioritizes which new experiences to encode into long-term memory, just as curiosity-driven intrinsic reward frameworks prioritize which unexplored states to learn from. This creates a new framing: active agent memory can be reframed as a persistent curiosity-driven selection problem, rather than just a storage or retrieval optimization problem.
